markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's confirmation of GTA VI's November 19, 2026 release date after two prior delays from the original Fall 2025 window has fixed a clear five-month horizon for Polymarket traders assessing "What will happen before GTA VI?" outcomes. This timeline intensifies focus on time-bound cultural and geopolitical catalysts, including major music drops like potential Playboi Carti or Drake projects that align with Rockstar's history of high-profile soundtrack integrations and marketing tie-ins. Trader consensus reflects the game's massive cultural weight, with recent Take-Two earnings commentary and insider reports underscoring steady development momentum and an expected marketing surge, while unrelated events such as a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire resolution carry high implied probabilities due to their independent timelines. Upcoming signals like a potential third trailer or pre-order announcements could further shift sentiment by highlighting promotional activity ahead of launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent repeated mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, most recently on May 15, 2026, for the Jessica Mann rape charge have left prosecutors weighing whether to pursue a fourth trial at the June 24 hearing. This pattern of deadlocked juries, combined with Weinstein’s existing California 16-year sentence (currently under appeal) and reports of his declining health, has driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” as the dominant outcome at 82.4%. Market-implied odds reflect skepticism that additional consecutive time will be imposed, especially given time already served and the high bar for further convictions in a case marked by procedural reversals. Upcoming decisions on retrial or plea offers represent the key near-term catalysts that could shift these probabilities.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the 2026 World Cup winner market as co-favorites due to deep squads, recent major-tournament pedigree including Spain’s Euro 2024 title, and star attackers like Lamine Yamal. Portugal, England, Argentina, and Brazil follow closely, supported by veterans such as Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi alongside strong midfield and defensive cores. The expanded 48-team format and early group-stage results have kept probabilities bunched, with several contenders still to play their openers. Multiple high-profile injuries, including absences for Brazil and the Netherlands, have created minor shifts without dramatically separating the top tier. Traders view the field as wide open given the parity among European and South American sides and the long path through knockout rounds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the 2026 World Cup winner market as co-favorites due to deep squads, recent major-tournament pedigree including Spain’s Euro 2024 title, and star attackers like Lamine Yamal. markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s position as CCP General Secretary and paramount leader remains secure through at least the end of 2026, reflecting sustained elite cohesion and the absence of any credible mechanism or faction capable of forcing his exit. Since abolishing presidential term limits in 2018 and securing a third term in 2022, he has filled senior party and military posts with loyalists, rendering formal age norms such as “seven up, eight down” largely inoperative. Recent military leadership changes, including the early 2026 removal of senior generals, have been interpreted by observers as further centralization rather than evidence of instability. No designated successor has emerged ahead of the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027, and public reporting shows no organized internal opposition or health developments that would trigger a leadership transition before the market’s December 31, 2026 cutoff. Trader consensus at 93.3% “No” aligns with this pattern of consolidated authority and the low historical frequency of abrupt removals in the post-Mao era.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the lead in the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market due to his California governorship, national visibility, and recent positioning moves such as a new memoir, media appearances, AI regulation efforts, and public acknowledgment of White House ambitions ahead of the 2026 midterms. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows as the top progressive option, drawing trader support from younger and left-leaning voter bases without formal declarations from any contender. The fragmented field reflects differences in name recognition, fundraising, Senate or gubernatorial records, and perceived general-election viability among figures like Jon Ossoff, Kamala Harris, and Josh Shapiro. Upcoming midterm results, early polling trends, and potential consolidation around electability or ideological lanes could shift probabilities before primary season begins.
